updates.tweet_text:
Last night we got the standard Sunday gap down into Failed Breakdown in #ES_F (my absolute favorite trade). We swept Friday's 7521 low to 7514, recovered, ripped +50.

Now, nothing to do. 7590, 7600, 7611 if ES wants more. No good supports until 7535 now (watch traps there) https://t.co/NXg3StXBks
updates.notes: 7600 is conversational upside reference; 7590, 7611, 7535 all in morning plan. 7521/7514 are observations of price action swept. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
There's 7590 1st target for today hit in #ES_F and now +70 points from last nights textbook "gap down into Failed Breakdown" long of Friday's 7521 low. My favorite setup, by far.

Targets were 7590 (hit), 7600, 7611. Ride runner. Then we see 7620, 7640+. No change to supports https://t.co/veK7GYRTlE
updates.notes: All levels cited (7590, 7600, 7611, 7620) are morning plan reaffirmations or target references; 7640 is casual upside commentary, not explicitly named as a new actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch The 7521 low definitely more important, it was the major low from which that whole rally began and it was a daily low which is is always the highest quality
updates.notes: Reply tweet referencing 7521 as historical context/observation of a prior low — no actionable naming language, no entry/watch/trigger directive.

updates.tweet_text: @JohnnyComeL8Lee ES is notoriously predictable. Almost never does anything surprising. Not all assets are the same though, commodities and even NQ are much more random
updates.notes: Pure conversational commentary comparing asset classes; no price levels named or actionable triggers mentioned.

updates.tweet_text:
Last entry was the 8:04PM 7521 Friday low Failed Breakdown in #ES_F. Today's 1st target was 7590 and we ran 70 points and hit it. Some mild volatility expansion now but no new entries until far lower

7528/35=support (watch traps of 7528). 7509 below. Same upside targets https://t.co/n5BFXv2xey
updates.notes: 7528/35 and 7509 are all morning-plan levels (7535, 7509 already published); 7590 is a reaffirmation. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @Jbuehler777 Yes, very nice failed breakdown. I didn't take it myself as I already got my points last night but huge follow through for anyone who did.
updates.notes: Reply to user commenting on a failed breakdown; no price levels named, purely conversational trade commentary.

updates.tweet_text:
Nice fast Failed Breakdown in #ES_F. 7535/7528 was the support as posted. I posted watch for traps of 7528. We swept below 7528, recovered for a Failed Breakdown, and ran +28. 7558, 7570, 7590 if ES wants more today.

7528/35 fails (weak zone now), 7509 is next down https://t.co/FAmrn7ahD0
updates.notes: All prices (7535, 7528, 7558, 7570, 7590, 7509) are morning-plan levels or observations of completed price action; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text:
Now its pure mid-day chop and as always, we do not trade chop. Trades only happen on massive volatility expansions, then on Failed Breakdowns. 7528 Failed Breakdown was long trigger. We swept it, and ran +28.

7558, 7570, 7590 if ES wants more. 7535/28=support. Weak. 7509 below https://t.co/LqRWyW6jrZ
updates.notes: 7528 is a hindsight FBD reference; 7558 is not in plan but listed casually as directional target; 7570/7590/7535/7509 are all morning-plan reaffirmations. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
After a pure chop day in #ES_F, overnight traders finally spoiled with vol. As always, I only trade vol, I do not trade chop. Per newsletter, the Failed Breakdown of 7427 triggered long, and we are +20

Plan today: 7427=support (weak now). 7462, 7472 above. bears control<7509 https://t.co/JjTdj84Sbv
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7427, 7462, 7472, 7509) are already in the morning plan. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @zero_baalance Was a good long! We had a fairly significant breakdown though so hopefully we can see some more red. Its been a bad few weeks with lack of volatility and too much green, making it tough for day traders. Need that red so we can grow accounts too (day traders always prefer red)
